NCT00830622.name: WelTel Kenya 1
NCT00830622.group: WelTel Kenya 1: weekly SMS check-in ("Mambo?" / "How are you?") with optional response from clinician for ART-adherent patients in 3 Kenyan HIV clinics. Lester RT et al. Lancet 2010;376:1838-1845. PMID 21071074. DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(10)61997-6. tE=168/273 (62%) adherent at 12 mo on intervention vs cE=132/263 (50%) standard care. RR 1.23 (95% CI 1.06-1.43; P=0.006). Defined the field of mHealth-supported ART adherence — extensively replicated post-2010 in LMIC settings.

allOutcomes.title: ART adherence >=95% by self-report at 12 months, SMS vs standard care

NCT00830622.snippet: Source: Lester RT, Ritvo P, Mills EJ, et al. Effects of a mobile phone short message service on antiretroviral treatment adherence in Kenya (WelTel Kenya1): a randomised trial. Lancet 2010;376:1838-1845. PMID 21071074.

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 538 ART-naive adults initiating treatment at 3 University-of-Nairobi-affiliated HIV clinics (Mbagathi, Mathari, Embakasi) between 2007-05 and 2008-09. Randomized 1:1 unblinded to weekly SMS check-in (n=273) vs standard care (n=263). Followed for 12 months with HIV-1 RNA at 6 and 12 months. Originating mHealth ART trial; spawned the WelTel platform deployed across multiple LMIC settings.

evidence.label: Primary outcome — adherence
evidence.text: Adherence >=95% by self-report at 12 months: intervention 168/273 (62%) vs control 132/263 (50%). RR 1.23 (95% CI 1.06-1.43; P=0.006). HIV-1 RNA suppression <400 copies/mL at 12 months: intervention 156/200 (78%) vs control 128/192 (67%). RR 1.16 (95% CI 1.04-1.29). The first RCT to demonstrate that simple SMS check-in improves ART outcomes — drove WHO guidance on mHealth in HIV care (2016+).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW. D2 SOME (unblinded by design — SMS visible). D3 LOW (95% retention). D4 LOW (central HIV-1 RNA). D5 LOW. Self-reported adherence has known limitations but VL endpoint is objective.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* HIV infected and starting antiretroviral therapy~* Adequate (daily) access to a cell phone~* Intending to attend the enrollment clinic for 2 years~* Consent to participate~Exclusion Criteria:~* Not meeting inclusion criteria"

title: RapidMeta Digital-Health | mHealth SMS for ART Adherence v0.1 (WelTel Kenya, post-2010)
